concerns.text: FR-004 defines inconsistency as "when the absolute difference between the reported p‑value and the reconstructed p‑value exceeds 0.05 **or** when the reported confidence interval does not contain the effect size implied by the reconstructed test." The reconstructed p‑value is computed from the *same* reported effect size and sample sizes that are used as inputs, i.e., it is a deterministic function of the reported metrics. Because the validation target (reported p‑value) is derived from the same quantities used to predict it, the validation is circular: the test cannot detect inconsistencies that arise from mis‑reporting of the underlying raw data (e.g., conversion counts) and may flag differences that are merely rounding artefacts. This violates independence between predictors and validation outcome, making the core claim of statistical inconsistency unsound.
